selected_plan.title: Unify asset visuals with a shared SwiftUI ticker badge
selected_plan.summary: Create one reusable circular AssetBadgeView, integrate it into Explore and share surfaces, then verify build, visual legibility, accessibility, and absence of official logo assets.

retained_alternatives.title: Circular badges with abstract patterns
retained_alternatives.summary: Add sector or asset-class patterns behind ticker text if color plus ticker proves too plain.
cons: More QA burden in compact surfaces

retained_alternatives.title: Rounded-square hero badges
retained_alternatives.summary: Use larger rounded-square badges in share or hero surfaces if circle readability is insufficient later.
pros: More room for long ticker text

leaf_tasks.title: Create the shared AssetBadgeView component
leaf_tasks.description: Add a SwiftUI component that renders a circular badge for any AssetID using its tint and symbol, with compact, standard, and hero sizing.
leaf_tasks.why: All screens need one consistent badge implementation instead of ad hoc circles, dots, and first-letter fallbacks.
done_criteria: AssetBadgeView exists under the shared feature layer
The component supports compact, standard, and hero sizes
The badge uses asset.tint and asset.symbol
Long symbols scale without overflowing the circular badge
The badge exposes an accessibility label containing symbol and display name
No PNG assets or official logos are introduced

verification.description: Build the app target after adding the component.
Confirm the component has no official logo or image asset dependency.

leaf_tasks.title: Integrate AssetBadgeView into ExploreView
leaf_tasks.description: Replace ExploreView asset dots and first-letter badge with the shared badge, and enrich the menu Picker asset rows where SwiftUI supports it.
leaf_tasks.why: The main simulation surface is where users most often see selected and compared assets.
done_criteria: ResultSummaryCard no longer uses symbol.prefix(1) as a badge
Comparison rows use compact AssetBadgeView instead of a small tint dot
Timeline legend uses compact AssetBadgeView instead of a small tint dot
ScenarioEditorCard keeps the menu Picker and includes badge treatment where practical
Existing scenario selection behavior is unchanged

verification.description: Build the WhatIfInvest app target for iOS simulator.
Inspect the Explore screen with a primary scenario and at least one comparison.

leaf_tasks.title: Integrate AssetBadgeView into ShareCardView
leaf_tasks.description: Use the shared badge in the share card primary asset area and comparison rows while preserving the poster-like numeric hierarchy.
leaf_tasks.why: Share surfaces should use the same asset identity language as the app without becoming cluttered or logo-dependent.
done_criteria: Primary share-card asset area includes a hero-size AssetBadgeView
Comparison rows use compact or standard AssetBadgeView instead of tint-only dots
Current share-card title, metrics, disclaimer, and data-refresh copy remain intact
Badge sizing does not clip in exported share-card rendering

verification.description: Build the WhatIfInvest app target for iOS simulator.
Render or preview the share card and inspect badge placement and clipping.

leaf_tasks.title: Verify build, visual fit, accessibility, and asset hygiene
leaf_tasks.description: Run the final checks needed to confirm the badge system is safe to ship and does not introduce official-logo assets.
leaf_tasks.why: This is a UI consistency change, so code review alone is not enough; compact ticker legibility and share rendering need visual validation.
done_criteria: The app builds successfully for iOS simulator
GOOGL, AMZN, and NVDA badges remain legible enough in compact contexts
Accessibility labels identify assets without relying only on color
Repository search shows no newly added official logo PNGs
Share card rendering is visually acceptable

verification.description: Run xcodebuild for the WhatIfInvest simulator target.
Inspect Explore and share-card surfaces on simulator or previews.
Search for newly added PNG logo assets or official brand-logo references.

risk_summary.description: Four- and five-letter symbols may be hard to read inside compact circular badges.
risk_summary.mitigation: Use size-specific fonts, minimumScaleFactor, and visual checks on GOOGL, AMZN, and NVDA.

risk_summary.description: SwiftUI menu Picker may not render complex custom row content consistently.
risk_summary.mitigation: Keep the menu Picker and only enrich rows in the simplest stable way; do not replace the control.

risk_summary.description: Share-card export can reveal clipping or scaling issues not obvious in regular app layout.
risk_summary.mitigation: Render or preview the share card after integration.
